name: Stand-Up India Scheme
administered_by: Department of Financial Services, Ministry of Finance (SIDBI - Stand-Up India portal); implemented through scheduled commercial bank branches
retrieval_text: Composite bank loan (term loan plus working capital) between Rs 10 lakh and Rs 1 crore for at least one Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch, to set up a new (greenfield) enterprise in manufacturing, services, trading, or allied agricultural activities. The borrower must hold at least 51% shareholding or controlling stake. Interest is capped at the bank's lowest applicable rate for that credit category, not exceeding MCLR plus 3% plus tenor premium, and eligible loans can be extended without collateral under the associated credit guarantee mechanism. Repayable over up to 7 years with a moratorium of up to 18 months.
